one_time_description: Agricultural Benefits Settlement received in prior year (2021: $136,431,042); in 2022 the Trust generated $3,277,195 net income distributed to the Nation
economic_notes: Subsequent to year end, the Nation purchased an investment in Nautical Energy Ltd. for $1,000,000. The Nation operates Sucker Creek Holdings Ltd., Sucker Creek Gas Utility, Sucker Creek First Nation Co-op, SCFN Services Limited Partnership, and SCFN Ventures Limited Partnership. The Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust holds $87,132,220 in investments. COVID-19 additional funding of $2,674,624 received from ISC in 2022.
notes: The 2021 comparative year includes $136,431,042 in Agricultural Benefits Settlement revenue and $86,204,219 in related disbursements, making prior year figures non-comparable. The Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust is fully consolidated (Note 1) and holds $83,889,427 as investment on the balance sheet. Five GBEs are accounted for under the modified equity method. The Agricultural Benefits Settlement Loan of $34,847,147 (RBC demand loan at prime) is a major liability related to the settlement. No schedule of remuneration for Chief and Council was included in the document provided. OSR calculated as total revenue ($37,441,011) minus ISC ($21,553,546), LSLIRC ($872,058), Government of Alberta ($654,440), FNDF ($583,154), and CMHC ($1,591,425) = $12,186,388; note that LSLIRC and FNDF transfers are included in government transfers total.
